name: End-to-End & Integration Tests
description: Playwright e2e test suite and API integration tests for OpenClaw Mission Control. Covers: login flow, overview page load (<2s), agent list rendering, project CRUD flow, task board interactions (create, move, assign), PACT pipeline view for fixture projects, activity feed SSE updates, and cost page rendering. All tests run against fixture data (mock openclaw.json, mock PACT directories, seeded SQLite) — no running OpenClaw instance or PACT daemon required. Targets Chromium at minimum with Firefox and WebKit configs. Includes integration tests for all API routes with Zod schema validation of responses.

types.description: Supported Playwright browser projects for cross-browser testing

types.name: TestTag

types.name: TestStatus

types.description: Generic wrapper for successful API responses validated in integration tests

functions.name: globalSetup
functions.description: Playwright global setup function. Copies fixture files to a temp directory, creates and seeds SQLite database from schema and seed SQL, sets environment variables pointing the Next.js test server at fixture data. Runs once before all test workers.

inputs.name: fixture_config
inputs.description: Paths to all fixture data sources

error_cases.name: fixture_not_found
error_cases.condition: Any configured fixture file or directory does not exist

error_cases.name: db_schema_error
error_cases.condition: Schema SQL is invalid or fails to execute

error_cases.name: db_seed_error
error_cases.condition: Seed SQL fails (constraint violation, syntax error)

error_cases.name: temp_dir_creation_failed
error_cases.condition: Cannot create temp directory (permissions, disk space)

preconditions: Fixture files exist at configured paths (openclaw.json, pact/, seed.sql, schema.sql)
No prior test environment is active (clean state)
postconditions: Temp directory created with copies of all fixture files
SQLite database created, schema applied, and seed data inserted
Environment variables OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH, PACT_DIR, DATABASE_PATH are set
TestEnvironment returned with all paths resolved

structured_side_effects.description: Creates temp dir and copies all fixture files
Creates and seeds the test SQLite database
Sets environment variables for Next.js test server

performance_budget.complexity: O(n) where n = total fixture file size

functions.name: globalTeardown
functions.description: Playwright global teardown function. Removes temp directories, closes database connections, and cleans up environment variables. Runs once after all test workers complete.

inputs.name: environment
inputs.description: The test environment created during global setup

error_cases.name: cleanup_failed
error_cases.condition: Cannot remove temp directory (file locked, permissions)

error_data.note: Should log warning but not throw — teardown must be best-effort

preconditions: globalSetup completed successfully and returned a valid TestEnvironment
postconditions: Temp directory and all contents deleted
SQLite database file removed
Environment variables unset
No orphaned file handles or processes

structured_side_effects.description: Recursively deletes temp directory and contents
Removes test environment variables

functions.name: createWorkerDatabase
functions.description: Creates a per-worker copy of the seeded SQLite database to ensure parallel test isolation. Each Playwright worker gets its own database file.

inputs.name: source_db_path
inputs.description: Path to the seed-populated SQLite database from global setup

inputs.name: worker_id
inputs.description: Playwright worker identifier for unique naming

error_cases.name: source_db_not_found
error_cases.condition: Source database file does not exist

error_cases.name: copy_failed
error_cases.condition: Failed to copy database file (disk space, permissions)

preconditions: Source database file exists and is a valid SQLite database
Worker ID is non-empty and filesystem-safe
postconditions: New database file created at a worker-specific path
Database is a byte-for-byte copy of the source
Returned string is the absolute path to the worker database

structured_side_effects.description: Copies seeded database for parallel isolation

functions.name: seedDatabase
functions.description: Applies schema and seed data to a SQLite database file. Used by global setup and can be called in individual tests for reset.

inputs.name: db_path
inputs.description: Path to the target SQLite database file

inputs.name: schema_sql
inputs.description: SQL string for schema creation (CREATE TABLE statements)

inputs.name: seed_sql
inputs.description: SQL string for inserting fixture data

error_cases.name: schema_invalid
error_cases.condition: Schema SQL contains syntax errors or invalid statements

error_cases.name: seed_constraint_violation
error_cases.condition: Seed data violates schema constraints (FK, UNIQUE, NOT NULL)

error_cases.name: db_write_failed
error_cases.condition: Cannot write to database path

preconditions: db_path parent directory exists and is writable
seed_sql references only tables defined in schema_sql
postconditions: Database file created or overwritten at db_path
All schema tables exist
All seed data rows are inserted
Database is in WAL mode for concurrent read support

structured_side_effects.description: Creates/overwrites database with schema and seed data

functions.name: resetDatabase
functions.description: Resets a worker database to its seeded state by dropping all tables and re-applying schema and seed data. Used between tests that mutate data.

inputs.name: db_path
inputs.description: Path to the worker SQLite database file

inputs.name: schema_sql
inputs.description: Schema SQL to re-apply

inputs.name: seed_sql
inputs.description: Seed SQL to re-apply

error_cases.name: db_not_found
error_cases.condition: Database file does not exist at db_path

error_cases.name: db_locked
error_cases.condition: Database is locked by another process

error_cases.name: reset_failed
error_cases.condition: Schema or seed re-application fails

preconditions: No active transactions or open connections from other processes
postconditions: All existing tables dropped
Schema and seed data re-applied cleanly
Database state identical to post-seedDatabase state

structured_side_effects.description: Drops and recreates all tables with seed data

postconditions: Returned FixtureAgent has all required fields populated

Default status is 'active'

functions.description: Factory function to create a FixturePactPipeline with the standard PACT stages (Plan, Act, Check, Transform) and sensible defaults.

inputs.name: project_id
inputs.description: Project this pipeline belongs to

inputs.name: overrides
inputs.description: Partial FixturePactPipeline fields to override defaults

error_cases.name: empty_project_id
error_cases.condition: project_id is empty string

postconditions: Pipeline has at least 4 stages (plan, act, check, transform)
current_stage_index is within bounds of stages array

functions.name: setupFixtureDirectories
functions.description: Creates the mock PACT directory tree structure in the temp directory with pipeline.json and session files for fixture projects.

inputs.name: temp_dir
inputs.description: Root temp directory for fixture files

inputs.name: pipelines
inputs.description: List of FixturePactPipeline objects to write as fixture files

inputs.name: agents
inputs.description: List of FixtureAgent objects to include in mock openclaw.json

error_cases.name: temp_dir_not_found
error_cases.condition: temp_dir does not exist

error_cases.name: write_failed
error_cases.condition: Cannot write files to temp_dir (permissions, disk space)

error_cases.name: serialization_error
error_cases.condition: Pipeline or agent data cannot be serialized to JSON

postconditions: openclaw.json written at temp_dir/openclaw.json with serialized agents
PACT directories created at temp_dir/pact/<project_id>/ for each pipeline
pipeline.json written in each project directory
sessions/ subdirectory created in each project directory
Returned FixtureConfig has all paths correctly set

structured_side_effects.description: Writes mock OpenClaw config with agent definitions
Writes PACT pipeline JSON for each fixture project
Creates session directories for each fixture project

functions.name: createApiClient
functions.description: Creates a typed HTTP client for API integration tests. Uses native fetch. Validates responses against Zod schemas from lib/types/.

inputs.name: config
inputs.description: API client configuration with base URL and timeout

error_cases.name: invalid_base_url
error_cases.condition: base_url is not a valid URL

error_cases.name: connection_refused
error_cases.condition: Server is not running at base_url

error_cases.name: timeout
error_cases.condition: Request exceeds configured timeout_ms

preconditions: config.base_url is a valid HTTP(S) URL
postconditions: Returned dict contains methods: get, post, put, delete
Each method accepts path (str) and optional body (dict), returns StandardApiResponse
All responses are validated against StandardApiResponse or StandardApiError shape
Timeout is enforced via AbortController

structured_side_effects.description: Makes HTTP requests to the test server API endpoints

functions.name: createSSEClient
functions.description: Creates a fetch-based SSE client for integration testing of the /api/events endpoint. Parses event stream, type-checks payloads against SSETestEvent schema, and collects events until max_events or timeout.

inputs.name: config
inputs.description: SSE client configuration with URL, timeout, filters, and max events

error_cases.name: connection_failed
error_cases.condition: Cannot establish SSE connection to the endpoint

error_cases.name: timeout
error_cases.condition: Timeout exceeded while waiting for events

error_cases.name: invalid_event_payload
error_cases.condition: Received event data fails Zod schema validation

error_cases.name: stream_closed_unexpectedly
error_cases.condition: Server closes SSE stream before expected events received

preconditions: config.url is a valid HTTP(S) URL pointing to the SSE endpoint
postconditions: Returned dict contains methods: connect, disconnect, waitForEvent, collectEvents, getReceivedEvents
connect() establishes SSE connection and begins collecting events
waitForEvent(eventType, timeout) resolves with the first matching SSETestEvent or rejects on timeout
collectEvents(count, timeout) resolves with an array of SSETestEvent when count events received or timeout
getReceivedEvents() returns all collected SSETestEvent objects
disconnect() closes the connection and stops collection

structured_side_effects.description: Establishes persistent SSE connection to collect events

functions.name: runE2EOverviewPageTest
functions.description: E2e test: Navigates to the overview/home page, asserts it loads within 2000ms (AC1), verifies key dashboard widgets are rendered (agent summary, project list, activity feed preview, cost summary). Tagged @performance @smoke.

inputs.name: environment
inputs.description: Active test environment with server URL and fixture paths

error_cases.name: page_load_timeout
error_cases.condition: Page does not load within 30s navigation timeout

error_cases.name: performance_assertion_failed
error_cases.condition: Content not visible within 2000ms

error_cases.name: widget_not_rendered
error_cases.condition: One or more dashboard widgets not found in DOM

error_cases.name: data_mismatch
error_cases.condition: Rendered data does not match fixture data

preconditions: Next.js test server is running at environment.base_url
Fixture data is loaded (agents, projects, tasks)
postconditions: Overview page loaded and primary content visible within 2000ms
Agent summary widget displays correct count from fixture data
Project list shows seeded projects
Activity feed preview shows recent entries
Cost summary widget renders without error
TestResult captures PerformanceMetrics

structured_side_effects.description: Navigates browser to overview page and loads all resources
Reads performance timing entries for assertions

error_cases.name: auth_not_persisted
error_cases.condition: Auth state lost on subsequent navigation

postconditions: Login form renders with email and password fields
Invalid input shows validation errors
Valid credentials redirect to dashboard
Auth cookie/token is set after successful login
Subsequent navigation retains authenticated state

structured_side_effects.description: Navigates through login flow and subsequent pages

functions.name: runE2EAgentListTest
functions.description: E2e test: Navigates to the agents page, verifies all fixture agents are rendered with correct names, roles, statuses, and model info. Tests filtering and sorting if available.

inputs.name: environment
inputs.description: Active test environment

inputs.name: expected_agents
inputs.description: List of FixtureAgent objects expected to be rendered

error_cases.name: agent_list_empty
error_cases.condition: Agent list renders but shows no agents

error_cases.name: agent_data_mismatch
error_cases.condition: Rendered agent data does not match fixture data

error_cases.name: missing_agent
error_cases.condition: One or more expected agents not found in rendered list

preconditions: Fixture openclaw.json has been loaded with agent data
postconditions: Agent list page renders without errors
Each expected agent appears with correct name, role, status, and model
Agent count matches expected_agents length
Agent status indicators use correct visual styling (colors, icons)

structured_side_effects.description: Navigates to agent list page

functions.name: runE2EProjectCRUDTest
functions.description: E2e test: Tests the full project CRUD lifecycle through the UI — create a new project, verify it appears in the list, edit its name and description, verify changes persist, delete it, and verify removal. Covers AC for project management.

inputs.name: environment
inputs.description: Active test environment

inputs.name: new_project
inputs.description: Project data to create during the test

error_cases.name: create_form_error
error_cases.condition: Project creation form fails to submit or shows unexpected error

error_cases.name: project_not_in_list
error_cases.condition: Created project does not appear in project list

error_cases.name: edit_not_persisted
error_cases.condition: Edited project fields revert on page reload

error_cases.name: delete_not_confirmed
error_cases.condition: Delete confirmation dialog not shown

error_cases.name: project_not_removed
error_cases.condition: Deleted project still appears in list

postconditions: Create: New project form submits successfully, project appears in list
Read: Project detail page shows correct name, description, status
Update: Edit form saves changes, list reflects updated name/description
Delete: Confirmation dialog shown, project removed from list after confirm
Database state reflects all CRUD operations

structured_side_effects.description: Navigates through project CRUD UI flow
CRUD operations modify database state via API

functions.name: runE2ETaskBoardTest
functions.description: E2e test: Tests task board interactions — create a task, move it between columns (todo → in_progress → review → done) via drag-and-drop or UI controls, assign it to an agent, verify visual updates. Covers AC for task board.

inputs.name: environment
inputs.description: Active test environment

inputs.name: project_id
inputs.description: Project ID whose task board to test

inputs.name: new_task
inputs.description: Task data to create during the test

error_cases.name: task_creation_failed
error_cases.condition: Task form submission fails or task not visible in board

error_cases.name: drag_drop_failed
error_cases.condition: Drag and drop interaction does not move task to target column

error_cases.name: assignment_failed
error_cases.condition: Agent assignment UI does not work or assignment not reflected

error_cases.name: state_not_persisted
error_cases.condition: Task position or assignment lost on page reload

preconditions: Project with project_id exists in seeded data
At least one agent exists for assignment
postconditions: Task created and visible in the 'todo' column
Task successfully moved to 'in_progress' column
Task successfully moved to 'review' column
Task successfully moved to 'done' column
Task assigned to an agent, agent avatar/name shown on task card
Task counts update in column headers
State persists on page reload

structured_side_effects.description: Navigates and interacts with task board UI
Task create, move, and assign modify database via API

functions.description: E2e test: Navigates to the PACT pipeline view for a fixture project, verifies all pipeline stages are rendered with correct names and statuses, verifies active stage is visually highlighted, and stage artifacts are listed. Tests fixture project-alpha pipeline.

inputs.description: Active test environment

inputs.name: expected_pipeline

error_cases.name: pipeline_not_rendered

error_cases.name: stage_count_mismatch

error_cases.name: active_stage_not_highlighted

preconditions: PACT fixture directory exists with pipeline.json for the project
postconditions: Pipeline view page renders without errors
All stages displayed in correct order with correct names
Each stage shows correct status (pending/active/completed/failed)
Active stage is visually distinguished (highlighted, expanded)
Stage artifacts listed for completed stages
Agent assignment shown for each stage

structured_side_effects.target: Next.js test server PACT pipeline page
structured_side_effects.description: Navigates to PACT pipeline view
Server reads PACT pipeline.json from fixture directory

functions.name: runIntegrationProjectsAPITest
functions.description: Integration test: Tests /api/projects CRUD operations. GET returns all projects, POST creates a project (validates Zod schema), PUT updates project fields, DELETE removes a project. Tests both success and error responses against StandardApiError schema.

inputs.name: api_client
inputs.description: Typed API client configured for the test server

inputs.name: test_project
inputs.description: Project data for create/update operations

error_cases.name: unexpected_status_code
error_cases.condition: API returns unexpected HTTP status for a given operation

error_cases.name: response_schema_mismatch
error_cases.condition: Response body does not match expected Zod schema

error_cases.name: missing_error_code
error_cases.condition: Error response lacks required code field

preconditions: API client is configured and connected
postconditions: GET /api/projects returns 200 with array of projects matching seed data
POST /api/projects with valid body returns 201 with created project
POST /api/projects with invalid body returns 400 with StandardApiError (VALIDATION_ERROR)
PUT /api/projects/:id with valid body returns 200 with updated project
PUT /api/projects/:nonexistent returns 404 with StandardApiError (NOT_FOUND)
DELETE /api/projects/:id returns 200 or 204
DELETE /api/projects/:nonexistent returns 404
All success responses validate against project Zod schema
All error responses validate against StandardApiError schema

structured_side_effects.description: Makes CRUD HTTP requests to projects API
Create, update, delete operations modify project data

functions.name: runIntegrationTasksAPITest
functions.description: Integration test: Tests /api/tasks CRUD operations. Validates task creation with project_id reference, status transitions, agent assignment, priority constraints, and error cases (invalid project_id, invalid status, etc.).

inputs.name: api_client
inputs.description: Typed API client configured for the test server

inputs.name: test_task
inputs.description: Task data for create/update operations

inputs.name: valid_project_id
inputs.description: ID of an existing project from seed data for FK reference

error_cases.name: fk_violation_not_caught
error_cases.condition: Invalid project_id accepted without error

error_cases.name: validation_bypass
error_cases.condition: Invalid priority or status values accepted

error_cases.name: response_schema_mismatch
error_cases.condition: Response body does not match expected Zod schema

preconditions: Database is in seeded state with at least one project and one agent
postconditions: GET /api/tasks returns 200 with array of tasks
GET /api/tasks?project_id=X filters by project
POST /api/tasks with valid body returns 201
POST /api/tasks with invalid project_id returns 400 or 404
POST /api/tasks with priority outside 1-5 returns 400 (VALIDATION_ERROR)
PUT /api/tasks/:id status field changes persist
PUT /api/tasks/:id assigned_agent_id field changes persist
DELETE /api/tasks/:id returns 200 or 204
All responses validate against appropriate Zod schemas

structured_side_effects.description: Makes CRUD HTTP requests to tasks API
Task CRUD operations modify database state

functions.name: runIntegrationAgentsAPITest
functions.description: Integration test: Tests /api/agents read-only operations. GET returns all agents from fixture openclaw.json. GET /:id returns a single agent. Tests 404 for non-existent agent ID. Validates response against agent Zod schema.

inputs.name: api_client
inputs.description: Typed API client configured for the test server

inputs.name: expected_agents
inputs.description: List of FixtureAgent objects expected from the API

error_cases.name: agent_count_mismatch
error_cases.condition: Number of returned agents does not match expected

error_cases.name: agent_data_mismatch
error_cases.condition: Agent fields do not match fixture data

error_cases.name: missing_404_on_nonexistent
error_cases.condition: Non-existent agent ID does not return 404

preconditions: Fixture openclaw.json is loaded with agent data
postconditions: GET /api/agents returns 200 with array matching expected_agents count
Each agent in response has id, name, role, status, model fields
GET /api/agents/:validId returns 200 with matching agent
GET /api/agents/:nonexistent returns 404 with StandardApiError
Agent data matches fixture openclaw.json values exactly
All responses validate against agent Zod schema

structured_side_effects.description: Makes GET requests to agents API
Server reads agent data from fixture file

functions.name: runIntegrationPactAPITest
functions.description: Integration test: Tests /api/pact read-only operations. GET /api/pact/pipelines returns all pipelines. GET /api/pact/pipelines/:projectId returns a specific pipeline. Tests graceful handling of missing/malformed pipeline.json files. Validates responses against PACT Zod schemas.

inputs.name: api_client
inputs.description: Typed API client configured for the test server

inputs.name: expected_pipeline
inputs.description: Expected pipeline data for the fixture project

error_cases.name: pipeline_not_found
error_cases.condition: Valid project but missing pipeline.json returns wrong status

error_cases.name: malformed_pipeline_crashes
error_cases.condition: Malformed pipeline.json causes server error instead of graceful 422/500

error_cases.name: stage_order_wrong
error_cases.condition: Pipeline stages returned in wrong order

error_cases.name: response_schema_mismatch
error_cases.condition: Response does not match PACT pipeline Zod schema

preconditions: PACT fixture directory exists with at least one valid pipeline.json
postconditions: GET /api/pact/pipelines returns 200 with array of pipelines
GET /api/pact/pipelines/:validProjectId returns 200 with pipeline matching expected_pipeline
GET /api/pact/pipelines/:nonexistent returns 404 with StandardApiError
Pipeline response includes stages array with correct order and status
Malformed pipeline.json returns 500 or 422 with descriptive error (never crashes server)
All valid responses validate against PACT pipeline Zod schema

structured_side_effects.description: Makes GET requests to PACT API
Server reads pipeline.json from fixture PACT directories

functions.name: runIntegrationSSEEndpointTest
functions.description: Integration test: Tests /api/events SSE endpoint. Verifies connection establishment, event stream format (event: type, data: JSON, id: optional), event filtering by type parameter, and graceful reconnection. Uses the fetch-based SSE test client.

inputs.name: sse_client_config
inputs.description: SSE client configuration for the test

inputs.name: api_client
inputs.description: API client to trigger events (e.g., create task to generate task_update event)

error_cases.name: connection_not_sse
error_cases.condition: Endpoint does not return text/event-stream content type

error_cases.name: event_not_received
error_cases.condition: Triggered action does not produce expected SSE event within timeout

error_cases.name: event_format_invalid
error_cases.condition: SSE event does not follow event: / data: / id: format

error_cases.name: filter_not_applied
error_cases.condition: Events of filtered-out types are still received

error_cases.name: event_payload_invalid
error_cases.condition: Event data JSON does not validate against schema

preconditions: Next.js test server is running with SSE endpoint active
postconditions: SSE connection established successfully (200 with text/event-stream content-type)
Connection sends proper SSE headers (Cache-Control: no-cache, Connection: keep-alive)
Triggered actions produce SSE events with correct type and data
Event data validates against SSETestEvent schema
Event filter parameter limits received events to specified types
Client disconnect is clean (no server errors)

structured_side_effects.description: Establishes SSE connection and receives events
Creates task to trigger SSE event
Task creation modifies database to trigger events

performance_budget.complexity: O(n) where n = number of events collected

functions.name: getPlaywrightConfig
functions.description: Returns the Playwright configuration object. Defines multi-browser projects (Chromium required, Firefox and WebKit optional), webServer config pointing at Next.js dev server, global setup/teardown paths, test directory patterns, reporter config, and retry/timeout settings.

inputs.name: ci_mode
inputs.description: Whether running in CI (enables all browsers, stricter timeouts, screenshot on failure)

inputs.name: base_url
inputs.description: Base URL for the Next.js test server

error_cases.name: invalid_base_url
error_cases.condition: base_url is not a valid URL

postconditions: Returned config includes at least 'chromium' project
In CI mode, 'firefox' and 'webkit' projects are also included
webServer config starts Next.js on the specified port
Global setup points to tests/global-setup.ts
Global teardown points to tests/global-teardown.ts
Test match patterns include tests/e2e/**/*.spec.ts and tests/integration/**/*.spec.ts
Timeout set to 30s per test, navigation timeout 15s
Retries set to 2 in CI, 0 locally
Screenshot on failure enabled in CI

data_access_layer: All data access via lib/data/ layer
Filesystem reads for OpenClaw and PACT data
sse_event_bus: Event types: agent_update, task_update, project_update, pact_update, activity

invariants: All tests run against fixture data only — no running OpenClaw instance or PACT daemon required
No test may import or require openclaw or pact as direct library dependencies
All API response assertions validate against the same Zod schemas used in production route handlers
All error responses conform to the StandardApiError shape with a typed ErrorCode
Database state is isolated per Playwright worker via per-worker database copies
Global setup must complete before any test worker starts
Global teardown must run even if tests fail (best-effort cleanup)
E2e tests use Page Object Models for all page interactions — no raw selectors in test bodies
Performance-tagged tests (@performance) run only in Chromium project
PACT file parsers must handle missing or malformed files gracefully — never crash on bad data
SSE integration tests must verify both event format and payload schema
All factory functions produce valid fixture objects that pass their own Zod schemas
Test fixtures are read-only during test execution — mutations go through API routes to the worker database
Chromium browser project is always required; Firefox and WebKit are optional in local dev
Tests must not depend on execution order — each test is independently runnable
No duplicate type definitions — all types imported from lib/types/ or defined in this test component
Files must stay under 250 lines per project standards; test helpers and POMs split into separate modules
